metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Instrumental Music 10 develops students' technical proficiency, musicianship, and ensemble skills on their chosen instrument. Students engage in regular performance practice, music literacy, sight-reading, and the study of repertoire from diverse musical traditions. Critical listening, personal reflection, and collaborative rehearsal are central to the course. First Peoples musical traditions and instruments are explored with appropriate cultural respect. This course opens pathways to Instrumental Music 11 and 12.
metadata.general_description: A senior instrumental music course developing technical proficiency, music literacy, ensemble performance skills, and musical understanding — with an emphasis on rehearsal process, diverse repertoire, and the development of musicianship for advanced study and lifelong music participation.

big_ideas.statement: Music provides opportunities to gain insight into the perspectives and experiences of people from a variety of times, places, and cultures.
big_ideas.elaboration: Repertoire from different musical traditions provides windows into diverse cultural values, histories, and aesthetic sensibilities. Understanding the cultural context of music deepens interpretation and appreciation.

big_ideas.statement: Creative growth requires patience, readiness to take risks, and willingness to try new approaches.
big_ideas.elaboration: Musical development is a patient, sustained process of practice, refinement, and artistic exploration. Growth comes through deliberate practice, openness to challenge, and learning from mistakes.

big_ideas.statement: Music is a unique language for creating and communicating.
big_ideas.elaboration: Music communicates emotional, cultural, and conceptual meaning through organized sound — using a specialized vocabulary of pitch, rhythm, dynamics, and form that performers must develop fluency in.

big_ideas.statement: Individual and collective expression through music is rooted in history, culture, and community.
big_ideas.elaboration: All musical traditions — from Indigenous song to Western orchestral music to contemporary popular music — are expressions of the communities that created them, carrying meaning that extends beyond the notes themselves.

competencies.raw: Develop technical skills and musical understanding on an instrument; explore and apply elements of music to create and perform; use imagination and inquiry to explore musical ideas; create original musical ideas through improvisation and composition.
i_can_statements: I can develop technical proficiency on my instrument through consistent, focused practice.
I can apply music elements — rhythm, melody, harmony, dynamics, articulation — expressively in performance.
I can explore and create musical ideas through improvisation and short composition activities.
I can rehearse and perform music with musical intention and expression.

competencies.raw: Analyze and respond to music using music-specific vocabulary; apply feedback to improve musical performance; reflect on rehearsal and performance experiences; examine music in social, cultural, and historical context.
i_can_statements: I can analyze music using accurate terminology: form, style, texture, dynamics, and articulation.
I can apply feedback from conductors, peers, and self-assessment to improve my performance.
I can reflect on rehearsal and performance experiences to identify areas for artistic growth.
I can examine the social, cultural, and historical contexts that shape the music I perform.

competencies.raw: Perform prepared and sight-read music for audiences; document musical learning and growth; communicate musical ideas to peers and conductors.
i_can_statements: I can perform prepared repertoire and sight-read music for an audience with confidence.
I can document my musical learning through practice logs, reflective journals, and recordings.
I can communicate musical ideas clearly to peers, conductors, and audiences.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate responsibility in rehearsal and performance; respect self, ensemble, and audience; explore diverse musical traditions including First Peoples music; build community connections through music.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate responsibility and professionalism in ensemble rehearsal and performance.
I can engage respectfully with diverse musical traditions, including First Peoples music.
I can build community connections by performing for and with members of my school and broader community.

areas.name: Instrumental Technique
topics: Embouchure, bow technique, or hand position: instrument-appropriate technical fundamentals
Tone production: breath support, articulation, and intonation
Range development: extending upper and lower registers
Technical exercises: scales, arpeggios, long tones, and etudes
Warm-up routines and practice strategies

areas.name: Music Literacy
areas.raw: Notation reading, rhythmic and melodic skills.
topics: Notation: reading treble, bass, or alto clef at grade-appropriate levels
Rhythm: simple and compound time, syncopation, and rhythmic notation
Sight-reading: strategies and progressive practice
Musical terminology: dynamics, tempos, articulations, form markings, and Italian terms
Score study and rehearsal number navigation

areas.name: Ensemble Performance
areas.raw: Rehearsal skills, ensemble awareness, and concert performance.
topics: Ensemble skills: listening, balance, blend, intonation, and rhythmic precision
Rehearsal techniques: sectional practice, problem-solving passages, and conductor communication
Concert preparation: performance etiquette, stage presence, and program notes
Diverse repertoire: concert band/orchestra/jazz/chamber music from multiple cultures and periods
Solo and chamber performance opportunities

areas.name: Music History and Cultural Contexts
areas.raw: Diverse musical traditions, First Peoples music, and music history.
topics: Western music history overview: Baroque, Classical, Romantic, and Modern periods
First Peoples musical traditions: instruments, songs, ceremonial music, and protocols
World music traditions: music from African, Latin, Asian, and other global traditions
Contemporary popular music: jazz, rock, hip hop, and electronic music influences
Musical innovators: influential composers, performers, and conductors
